technical.torch_dependent_tests: run on GB10 (torch not installed in this CPU worktree); device-free logic is fully covered via monkeypatched _cuda_available
technical.lazy_ml_init: psalm.infrastructure.ml.__init__ made lazy (PEP 562) so torch-free modules import standalone

M-no-cpu.check: battery aborts (non-zero) when CUDA unavailable; no silent CUDA->CPU fallback
M-no-cpu.note: New device.py: require_cuda_device() raises CudaUnavailableError; resolve_training_device() never downgrades a requested cuda device to cpu (explicit cpu still allowed for proxy). trainer._resolve_device delegates to it; the old warn+cpu fallback is deleted. TrainConfig.device defaults to 'cuda', so the battery aborts on a CPU-only host. Covered by test_device.py.

M-no-mock.check: RunMode.MOCK / MockUniformBaseline removed from battery; smoke venues cannot emit a verdict
M-no-mock.note: MockUniformBaseline class and RunMode.MOCK enum member deleted; build_babylm_smoke_model is ELC-PSALM-only; SmokeEvalResult.evidence=False always; assert_evidence_grade() raises NotEvidenceGradeError on smoke. CLI --mock/--no-elc removed. Source scan (C6) confirms 0 occurrences in the battery.

M-paired-stats.check: paired bootstrap on per-seed differences matches hand-checked reference; independent-resample path removed
M-paired-stats.note: paired_bootstrap() resamples per-seed diffs; paired_permutation_test() uses exact sign-flip randomization. _paired_or_independent_effect and the pooled two-sample permutation are deleted. Hand-checked refs: constant-diff bootstrap has zero-width CI at the mean; 2-seed sign-flip gives two-sided p≈0.5. go_no_go.decide_h1 now uses the paired test.

M-official-venue.check: official EWoK + full BLiMP-arg via invoke_official_zero_shot; ELC-PSALM PLL (not causal logprob)
M-official-venue.note: OFFICIAL_TASKS=('blimp','blimp_supplement','ewok'); invoke_official_zero_shot validates task and rejects any non-'mlm' backend (ELC-PSALM is a masked LM); invoke_official_battery runs the full venue set. Subprocess invocation requires the installed pipeline + GB10 for real numbers (Phase 4).

M-leakage.check: train/eval string-set disjointness check fails closed on overlap
M-leakage.note: leakage.assert_disjoint() raises LeakageError on any whitespace-normalized overlap; check_disjoint() reports counts + examples. Covered by test_leakage.py.

M-seeds.check: gating contrasts use >=10 seeds (n=3 only for wiring smokes)
M-seeds.note: MIN_GATING_SEEDS=10; decide_h1 fails closed below it unless wiring_smoke=True, which stamps evidence=False on the H1Decision. Mismatched arm lengths also fail closed.

M-info-parity.check: information-parity audit returns PASS/FAIL across the six ADR-0035 D6 checks
M-info-parity.note: information_parity.audit_information_parity evaluates C1 H(struct|karaka)>=1 bit, C2 VISAYATA==1.0, C3 templates>=10, C4 accept>=target, C5 venue in [0.55,0.75], C6 mock/CPU paths==0 (via scan_mock_cpu_paths over the battery source). scripts/run_info_parity_audit.py wires the paribhāṣā parity report + venue measurement + source scan and exits non-zero on FAIL (blocks GPU training at GATE 3). A demonstrative run with the paribhāṣā-core numbers (H=3.18, VISAYATA=1.0, templates=499, accept=0.81, venue=0.65) returns PASS with C6=0.

gpu_validation: pending GB10: torch is not installed in this CPU worktree; the official-shard PLL smoke and the live GPU abort-without-CUDA check run during the GATE-3-gated rerun (Phase 4). The harness logic is proven device-free here.
gate: GATE 2 (per-workstream standalone closure); awaiting human sign-off. Note: this harness also feeds GATE 3, which gates whether GPU training may start.
